title: Phase 1 Directional Activation Approval
productPosture: Awareness Platform First, Route Intelligence Second

corridorApprovals.rationale: US 90 has a promotable inactive runtime subset and all required protections pass; approval carries observations for review-required volume, source metadata gaps, and cross-county/source containment controls.

TX 146 has a promotable inactive runtime subset and all required protections pass; approval carries observations for review-required volume, source metadata gaps, and cross-county/source containment controls.

Continued deferral remains appropriate because candidateCount=5, promotableCount=0, reviewRequiredCount=5, all candidates are manual-review-required, and the runtime candidate set remains empty by fail-closed design.

approvalMatrix.rationale: US 90 has a promotable inactive runtime subset and all required protections pass; approval carries observations for review-required volume, source metadata gaps, and cross-county/source containment controls.

TX 146 has a promotable inactive runtime subset and all required protections pass; approval carries observations for review-required volume, source metadata gaps, and cross-county/source containment controls.

Continued deferral remains appropriate because candidateCount=5, promotableCount=0, reviewRequiredCount=5, all candidates are manual-review-required, and the runtime candidate set remains empty by fail-closed design.

protectionReview.rationale: Protections remain unchanged and inactive; observations are limited to source metadata/county containment evidence, not runtime behavior.

riskReview.rationale: US 90 and TX 146 carry moderate evidence/maintenance risk; FM 1960 is high corridor-specific risk and remains deferred, reducing activation-package risk.

recommendation: Approve US 90 and TX 146 only; defer FM 1960.
finalRationale: Approval is limited to the activation package determination for US 90 and TX 146. No runtime activation, user-facing rendering, routing, navigation, county, Supabase, or protected-system changes are made. FM 1960 remains deferred because it has zero promotable candidates and all five candidates remain in the review-required bucket.
